label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the question is asking if following these instructions will make you return to the starting point. The instructions are: Turn left. Turn left. Turn left. That would make me face West (270 degrees). 2. Turn left again. Now facing South (180 degrees). 3. Take 10 steps South. 4. Take 6 steps South (since I'm still facing South after the previous step). So total of 16 steps South so far. 5. Turn left. Now facing East (90 degrees). 6. Take 3 steps East. 7. Take 3 steps East again. Total of 6 steps East. Now, I need to see if the East-West and North-South movements cancel each other out. In the North-South direction: I moved 16 steps South. There's no North movement, so net movement is 16 South. In the East-West direction: I moved 6 steps East. There's no West movement after the initial turns, so net movement is 6 East. Therefore, the total displacement is 16 South and 6 East from the starting point. So no, I don't return to the starting point. Therefore, the answer is No. Wait, but wait. Let me recheck.
